User Story: As a user, I want the app to appear as a tray icon on startup so I can reach it without a window.
Expected Behaviour: On launch a tray icon is created with the app icon and tooltip 'Epic Quick Switch'. Only a genuinely ACTIVE account puts its badge+name on the tray; when nobody is logged in the default icon and neutral tooltip show, so the tray never fakes a login.
Test Result: Fixed: logged-out state now visually distinct (default icon instead of last account's badge).
Notes: Manual check: log out in Epic -> tray falls back to app icon within ~3s.

User Story: As a user, I want the menu on left-click so I can switch with a single click path.
Expected Behaviour: show_menu_on_left_click(true): both left and right click open the tray menu.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want my accounts listed with the active one first and then most recently used, so the likely target is on top.
Expected Behaviour: list_accounts sorts: is_current first, then by last_used (fallback saved_at) descending.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want to see which account is active so I don't switch to it again.
Expected Behaviour: Active account label gets suffix '• active/aktiv', entry disabled. Additionally switch_account no-ops (AlreadyActive) when the target already owns the live session AND the launcher runs — so even a stale menu can't trigger a pointless kill+relaunch; with the launcher dead it falls through and relaunches.
Test Result: Fixed round 1 (id-level guard) + round 2 (guard requires running launcher, else full flow relaunches).

User Story: As a user, I want expired sessions marked so I know a switch will fail before I try.
Expected Behaviour: Accounts whose token was rejected get suffix '(expired/abgelaufen)'. Stale clears on re-save. Stale accounts refuse to switch (see F11) instead of logging the user out.
Test Result: Two accepted nuances: stale-mark save failure window (tiny, self-heals); 'active (expired)' can co-occur for undecryptable-but-current snapshots (both suffixes are true facts).

User Story: As a user, I want each account to have a recognizable colored badge so I can tell them apart at a glance.
Expected Behaviour: Deterministic palette color + white initial. Accented Latin initials are transliterated (Ü->U, é->E, ß->S, …); unmappable scripts fall through to the account id's first mappable char; badge initial follows the displayed label (see F20).
Test Result: Fixed: umlaut/accent names no longer render the '?' fallback glyph.

User Story: As a new user, I want a hint when no accounts are saved so I know how to start.
Expected Behaviour: With zero accounts the menu shows two disabled entries: 'No accounts saved yet' + login hint (localized).
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want to snapshot my current Epic login so I can switch back to it later.
Expected Behaviour: Reads [RememberMe] (launcher may keep running), requires valid token, account id from registry ONLY (fail-closed), name from logs by id (fallback 'Account <first8>'), DPAPI-encrypted store, tray refresh. Saving lifts a removal tombstone (see F12).
Test Result: Stale doc comment fixed. Remaining nuance accepted: no success toast on re-save (new-entry appearance is the feedback for first saves).

User Story: As a user, I want a clear error message when saving fails so I know how to fix it.
Expected Behaviour: NoLauncherData/NoSession/NoAccountId fully localized; Store failures show a localized headline with the technical reason as a detail line.
Test Result: Fixed: no more raw-English dialog bodies under German titles.

User Story: As a user, I want my manually chosen name kept, but generated names upgraded automatically when a real name appears.
Expected Behaviour: Custom names never overwritten; non-custom names upgraded from logs; re-save refreshes token and clears stale.
Test Result: Logic correct; known gap: no in-app rename UI (custom names require editing accounts.json). Feature request, not an error — a tray-only app needs a window/input dialog for it.

User Story: As a user, I want one click to switch Epic to another saved account without typing a password.
Expected Behaviour: Click: validate (exists, token valid, NOT stale — stale refuses with guidance), no-op if already active with launcher running, resolve exe+ini pre-kill, kill launcher+helpers (fail-closed check), snapshot+persist outgoing, write token (+mirror), best-effort registry, relaunch foreground, refresh. One switch at a time: further clicks get a localized 'switch in progress' notice. All failure dialogs fully localized.
Test Result: Fixed rounds 1-3: typed localized SwitchError; stale refusal; re-entrancy guard with busy feedback (panic-safe via Drop guard); generation claimed under the guard.
Notes: Manual E2E (destructive, not automated): switch between two saved accounts, verify launcher restarts in foreground logged into the target.

User Story: As a user, I want the account I'm leaving to keep a fresh token so switching back works.
Expected Behaviour: Post-kill, a valid live session of a NON-target account is snapshotted and PERSISTED before the target write — including never-saved accounts (no session silently lost). Exception: explicitly REMOVED accounts are tombstoned and never re-captured; a manual save lifts the tombstone.
Test Result: Fixed round 1 (auto-save unsaved outgoing) + round 2 (tombstones prevent resurrecting deliberately removed tokens).

User Story: As a user, I want unrelated Unreal/EOS apps untouched when the launcher is killed.
Expected Behaviour: Launcher-unique images killed by name; generic helpers by PID only under launcher tree/EOS dir. With the main process already dead, the sweep still runs — including EpicWebHelper.exe — so crash orphans can't stall the relaunch.
Test Result: Fixed round 1 (sweep on dead launcher) + round 2 (sweep includes launcher images, not only scoped helpers).

User Story: As a user, I want the launcher to open visibly after a switch so I see I'm logged in.
Expected Behaviour: Relaunch without '-silent': window opens in the foreground.
Test Result: match
Notes: Manual check: after a switch the Epic window appears (not just tray).

User Story: As a user, I want a clear error if the switch fails so I'm not left guessing.
Expected Behaviour: Every failure shows a localized dialog and refreshes the tray — including the stale-menu-entry case (account vanished), running-check failure, and post-kill failures (which leave the launcher closed; no rollback).
Test Result: Fixed: silent dead click eliminated; full error enumeration localized DE+EN.

User Story: As a user, I want to be told when Epic rejected the restored session so I can re-login and re-save.
Expected Behaviour: ~20s after a REAL switch (never after a no-op or failed preflight): LoggedOut + registry naming the switched-to account => mark stale, localized dialog, refresh. Newer switches supersede pending checks (generation claimed under the in-progress guard).
Test Result: Improved rounds 2-3: no bogus check after no-ops; generation ordering race closed. Accepted limitations: manual logout inside the 20s window is misread as rejection (re-save recovers); one-shot probe misses later rejections.

User Story: As a user, I want the switched-to account's display name to be current so a save right after the switch shows the right name.
Expected Behaviour: refresh_log_name(target_id, username_for(target_id)): id-matched, non-custom only, None is a no-op.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want to remove a saved account after confirmation, without touching the Epic account itself.
Expected Behaviour: Confirm dialog defaults to NO; Yes deletes the snapshot, tombstones the id (so switching away from it won't silently re-capture the token) and refreshes; vanished-account clicks give feedback. Epic account itself untouched.
Test Result: Fixed: No-default (MB_DEFBUTTON2), dead-click feedback, tombstone semantics.
Notes: Manual check: Enter on the confirm dialog must NOT delete.

User Story: As a user, I want the UI in German or English, defaulting to my OS language.
Expected Behaviour: EN/DE check items, persisted, validated, OS-locale fallback, immediate rebuild.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want to switch between Epic username and account ID display for privacy or clarity.
Expected Behaviour: 'Epic name' vs first-8-chars-of-ID for labels, remove submenu, tooltip AND badge initials — ID mode leaks no letter of the username anywhere.
Test Result: Fixed: badge initial now derives from the displayed label.

User Story: As a user, I want the app to start with Windows by default, but respect it when I turn that off.
Expected Behaviour: First-run default: enable + flag only on success (retried until it works). Any manual toggle marks autostart as configured — the default can never override an explicit user choice. Toggle failures show a localized dialog.
Test Result: Fixed rounds 1-2: success-gated flag; visible toggle errors; mark-configured on manual toggle (closes the re-enable-after-disable hole).

User Story: As a user, I want the tray to stay accurate when I log in/out in Epic directly.
Expected Behaviour: 3s poll of registry-id + ini mtime/size; refresh on change. Stale-menu races are harmless: switching to the already-active account no-ops.
Test Result: Fixed via F04's already-active guard.

User Story: As a user, I want the app to keep running without windows and quit only via the menu.
Expected Behaviour: No window; exit prevention; Quit waits for in-flight switch/store ops (busy lock, worker thread) and sets QUITTING so the auto-updater aborts a pending install instead of restarting; a quit during install exits instead of restarting.
Test Result: Fixed rounds 1-3: lock-gated quit; QUITTING flag checked before install AND after install (exit instead of restart).

User Story: As a user, I want only one instance so tray icons don't multiply.
Expected Behaviour: Second launch exits; the running instance shows a localized 'already running' info dialog (worker thread, non-blocking).
Test Result: Fixed: second launch is no longer a silent no-op.
Notes: Manual check: start the exe twice -> info dialog from the first instance.

User Story: As a user, I want the app to update itself from GitHub releases without breaking a switch in progress.
Expected Behaviour: Background check; download first; install+restart gated by the busy lock and the QUITTING flag.
Test Result: Accepted rare windows: a switch queued behind a running install is lost on restart; a pending 20s stale check dies on update restart. Both self-heal after restart.

User Story: As a user, I want my saved session tokens protected so other Windows users can't steal them.
Expected Behaviour: DPAPI current-user + app entropy, 'dpapi:' prefix, plaintext passthrough for recovery, Debug redaction.
Test Result: Match; accepted nuance: DPAPI-undecryptable snapshots share the '(expired)' label with rejected tokens — same user remedy (re-login + re-save).

User Story: As a user, I want the account store to survive crashes so I never lose all tokens at once.
Expected Behaviour: Temp+fsync+rename saves; corrupt files quarantined to numbered accounts.json.bad[.N] (earlier quarantines survive); undecryptable ciphertext preserved verbatim.
Test Result: Fixed: numbered quarantine. Accepted: quarantine itself is silent (a dialog in the constantly-running load path would spam; the .bad file is the recovery path).

User Story: As a user, I want simultaneous tray actions to never corrupt my saved accounts.
Expected Behaviour: Process-wide mutex over every load-mutate-save; gates updater install and quit; poison-recovering.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want the app to only touch the RememberMe token so the rest of my launcher config is untouched.
Expected Behaviour: Byte-identical round-trip outside [RememberMe] for uniform files (UTF-8/UTF-16LE ± BOM; dominant line terminator; mixed CRLF/LF normalized to dominant). Reads honor the LAST duplicate section (launcher last-wins parity); writes collapse duplicates. Atomic writes.
Test Result: Fixed: read/write duplicate-section asymmetry; wording corrected.

User Story: As a user, I want logged-out placeholders never treated as usable sessions.
Expected Behaviour: Valid = Enable=True + Data > 1000 chars; else LoggedOut; missing config = NoLauncherData.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want the app to find the right config regardless of launcher build.
Expected Behaviour: WindowsEditor > Windows candidates; RememberMe-bearing preferred; newer mtime tiebreak; mirror receives best-effort writes. Resolve failures reach the user localized (ConfigNotFound).
Test Result: Fixed via SwitchError localization.

User Story: As a user, I want switching to work even after the launcher self-updates or moved.
Expected Behaviour: Registry protocol handler, then default paths; missing exe aborts pre-kill with a localized dialog.
Test Result: Fixed via SwitchError localization.

User Story: As a user, I want my Epic username shown instead of a hex id whenever it is locally known.
Expected Behaviour: Newest-first log scan for -epicusername/-epicuserid pairs; id-matched, case-insensitive; fallback 'Account <first8>'.
Test Method: adversarial code-verify x2; unit; live data
Test Result: Match; known limitation (shared with F10): an account that never launches a game keeps the fallback label — rename UI is a feature request.

User Story: As a user, I want the app to track the active account the same way Epic does.
Expected Behaviour: HKCU Identifiers AccountId read (trimmed, empty->None); switch writes it best-effort; never trusted alone for 'active'.
Test Result: Accepted: a failed registry write stays invisible (ini token is authoritative; failing the switch over it would be worse). Doc comment now says 'best-effort'.

User Story: As a user, I want every label and dialog in my language.
Expected Behaviour: Complete DE+EN label sets covering menu, settings, info dialogs and ALL error dialog bodies (save, switch, autostart, busy, already-running); technical details appended as separate lines.
Test Result: Fixed: 12 new labels; no hardcoded-English dialog bodies remain.

User Story: As a user, I want a distinctive app icon that reflects account switching.
Expected Behaviour: Badge-swap icon as full Tauri set (.ico/PNGs/.icns/Squares); SVG source in repo.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want the app to treat differently-cased forms of the same account ID as one account so I never get duplicates.
Expected Behaviour: All store operations (incl. tombstones) match ids ASCII-case-insensitively; 'ABC' updates existing 'abc'.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want session tokens to never reach the UI layer so they cannot leak via tooltips or logs.
Expected Behaviour: UI Account struct carries no token field.
Test Result: match

User Story: As a user, I want updates cryptographically verified so a spoofed feed cannot install malware.
Expected Behaviour: Minisign-pinned updater; unsigned artifacts refuse to install.
Test Result: match
